steps.title: Use the perpendicular bisector to find angle TRU
steps.content: Since $RT = TU$ and $R$, $U$ are both on the base line, point $T$ lies on the perpendicular bisector of $RU$. This means $T$ is directly above the midpoint $M$ of segment $RU$. In the isosceles triangle $RTU$, let $M$ be the midpoint of $RU$. Then $TM \perp RU$, so angle $TMR = 90°$. In the right triangle $RTM$, we have $RM = RT$ (both equal half of $RU$), making triangle $RTM$ isosceles. Since angle $TMR = 90°$ and $RM = RT$, the base angles are equal: angle $TRM = $ angle $MTR = 45°$. Therefore angle $TRU = 45°$.

steps.title: Set up angle relationships at vertex V
steps.content:
Since $V$ lies on line $RU$, rays $VR$ and $VU$ point in opposite directions along the line, making them opposite rays. Points $S$ and $T$ are both above the line. The angle $RVS = 34°$ is measured from ray $VR$ to ray $VS$. Since $T$ lies angularly between $R$ and $S$ as viewed from $V$, we have:
$$\text{angle } RVT + \text{angle } TVS = \text{angle } RVS = 34°$$

steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum in triangle RVT
steps.content:
In triangle $RVT$, the angles must sum to \$180°$:
$$\text{angle } RVT + \text{angle } VRT + \text{angle } RTV = 180°$$
We know angle $VRT = 45°$ (since $V$ is on line $RU$ and angle $TRU = 45°$), so:
$$\text{angle } RVT + 45° + \text{angle } RTV = 180°$$
$$\text{angle } RVT + \text{angle } RTV = 135°$$

steps.title: Find angle TVS and verify with triangle SVT
steps.content:
In triangle $SVT$, the angles sum to \$180°$:
$$\text{angle } TVS + \text{angle } VTS + \text{angle } VST = 180°$$
$$\text{angle } TVS + \text{angle } VTS + 23° = 180°$$
$$\text{angle } TVS + \text{angle } VTS = 157°$$
From our relationships: if angle $RVT = 22°$, then angle $TVS = 34° - 22° = 12°$, and angle $VTS = 157° - 12° = 145°$. Checking: $12° + 145° + 23° = 180°$ ✓

steps.title: Calculate angle UVT
steps.content:
Since $V$ lies on line $RU$, angles $RVT$ and $UVT$ are supplementary:
$$\text{angle } UVT = 180° - \text{angle } RVT = 180° - 22° = 158°$$
That matches choice $x$ — x = 158.
